context:
Current State:
12-week remodel milestone plan, with weekly milestones: Week 1 — Finalize scope & kickoff; Week 2 — Permits, detailed scheduling & prep; Week 3 — Demo & investigative work; Week 4 — Rough-in systems & urgent repairs; Week 5 — Bathroom rebuild + kitchen cabinet work; Week 6 — Kitchen mid-build + tiles/fixtures; Week 7 — Countertop install window finishing; Week 8 — Trim/lighting & pre-finish prep; Week 9 — Drywall patching, primer, and prep for floors; Week 10 — Floor sanding & staining; Week 11 — Final coats & trim reinstall; Week 12 — Punch list, cleaning & closeout. Key notes: long-lead items ordered by Week 1–2; bathroom availability; inspections & permits timing; contingency buffers; dust sequencing; weekly updates with GC/contractors. Optional: convert into one-page checklist for contractors.

- Budgeting for waste removal and temporary storage:
  - Typical budget range: roughly $500–$1,500 for a modest kitchen/flooring refresh; low/high end: $150 up to $3,000 depending on scope and services.
  - Dumpster / debris removal
    - 10–15 yard roll-off: $250–$600
    - 20 yard roll-off: $350–$900
    - Extras: street permit $25–$150; landfill/tipping fees; overweight charges; contractor haul-away $100–$400
  - Full-service junk removal: $200–$800+
  - Hazardous or special disposal: $25–$200+; asbestos/lead abatement costs higher
  - Temporary storage
    - POD/mobile storage: delivery + 1–3 months rent + pickup: $200–$400/month; total ~$700–$1,500 for 3 months
    - Self-storage (10×10): $60–$180/month; 3 months $180–$540; plus truck or movers: $50–$150/day or $200–$600
    - Garage/spare room: could be free if available
  - Budgeting scenarios
    - Minimal/DIY: $150–$500
    - Moderate/typical: $500–$1,500
    - Conservative/worst-case: $1,500–$3,000+
  - Quick tips
    - Ask contractors whether demolition debris removal is included; get haul-away fees in bids
    - Check municipal bulk-pickup rules and landfill tipping fees
    - If renting a dumpster, size appropriately to avoid overspending or overage fees
    - If asbestos/lead suspected, test first; abatement affects costs and disposal rules
  - Optional: provide a short contractor checklist for removal/storage pricing comparisons

Subfloor repair guidance (new information):
- Subfloor under one patch of carpet felt a bit spongy in the hallway. If localized soft spots are found during demo, typical repair approach and cost range:
  1) Diagnose first
  - Locate and mark the soft area(s).
  - Confirm source: check for plumbing leaks, roof/downspout drainage, or high humidity. Use a moisture meter if available.
  - Pry up a small section of flooring/carpet to inspect the finish boards, underlayment/subfloor, and joists.
  2) Usual repair steps (small/localized)
  - Remove the damaged finish boards and the affected subfloor panel or planks down to the joists.
  - Dry and treat any mold or mildew. If wood is only slightly damp,
